data.content:
I can see you have an MSTest project with a failing test — `CalculateShipping_ExpressDelivery_Returns15` expects `15.00m` but the method returns `10.00m` for express.

I'll start `dotnet watch test` which will automatically re-run your tests whenever you save a file:

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to set up hot reload for iterating on failing tests. For .NET, this is done with `dotnet watch test`, which will re-run tests whenever files change.

Looking at the test file, I can see there's a failing test: `CalculateShipping_ExpressDelivery_Returns15` expects `15.00m` but the `CalculateShipping` method returns `10.00m` for "express".

Let me set up `dotnet watch test` for them. This is a built-in .NET SDK feature - no additional packages needed. I'll start it as an async detached process so it runs in the background.

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-6ffbb0043f1143bb8bc85188d1d36a47 && dotnet watch test --no-hot-reload 2>&1", "description": "Start dotnet watch test for hot reload", "mode": "async", "shellId": "dotnet-watch"}

toolArgs.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

data.content:
`dotnet watch test` is now running and watching for changes. Here's the current status:

- ✅ `CalculateShipping_StandardDelivery_Returns5` — passed
- ❌ `CalculateShipping_ExpressDelivery_Returns15` — failed (expected `15.00`, got `10.00`)

**Hot reload is active.** Every time you save a file, tests will automatically re-run. The watcher is running in shell `dotnet-watch`.

To fix the failing test, you'd change `"express" => 10.00m` to `"express" => 15.00m` in `ShippingCalculatorTests.cs` (line 24). Want me to fix it?
